action: Soak #3d cycles 311/312: claude-tmux scout/retro killed with exit=81/85 'bridge artifact timeout' while DEMONSTRABLY BUSY (tmux-final-scrollback shows the scout actively 'Schlepping 50s · ↑ 3.1k tokens' investigating the failure pile at kill time; headless `claude -p` probe succeeded immediately → NOT a quota wall). The StopReviewer extend-on-busy mechanism (ADR-0026, driver_tmux_repl.go ~line 426; EVOLVE_ARTIFACT_TIMEOUT_S default 300s interval, EVOLVE_ARTIFACT_MAX_EXTENDS default 6) SHOULD extend a busy agent — investigate why it did not: (a) does the StopReviewer's Busy/Progressed detection fire for claude 2.1.175's spinner chrome ('Schlepping Ns · ↑ Nk tokens'), or did the 2.1.17x chrome change slip past the busySpinnerStatsRE added in PR #76 (`00690de4`)? (b) was maxExtends (6×300s=30min) genuinely exhausted by an over-large scout task, or did a single interval kill it? FEEDBACK LOOP to fix at the root: each batch FAIL grows state.json:failedApproaches + the lessons corpus → the next scout reads MORE → runs slower → more likely to time out → more fails. Mitigations to evaluate: (1) verify/repair the busy-extend path for current claude chrome (highest value — a busy agent must never be killed); (2) cap the scout's failure-context intake (top-K recent failedApproaches, not all 32+); (3) prune expired failedApproaches more aggressively before batch start. TDD: a fixture replaying the 2.1.175 'Schlepping' busy frame must classify Busy and extend, not pause/kill.
evidence_pointer: go/internal/bridge/driver_tmux_repl.go (artifact-wait loop ~426); go/internal/bridge/stopreview.go (extend/pause adjudication); go/internal/bridge/fatalpane.go; PR #76 00690de4 (busySpinnerStatsRE); evolve-batch-w4soak3d-20260613.log cycles 311/312; .evolve/runs/cycle-312/tmux-final-scrollback.txt
